faers_data._source: FDA FAERS AERS legacy system extract (1999–2004 reporting period). Cardiovascular event counts sourced from Graham DJ et al. (2005) Lancet PMID:16260634 and the US Senate Finance Committee investigation report (2004). Estimated excess MI events 27,785–55,000 per Graham et al. post-marketing cohort.
faers_data.indication: Osteoarthritis, Acute Pain, Rheumatoid Arthritis, Primary Dysmenorrhea

cardiovascular_cluster.note: Unprecedented concentration of serious thrombotic cardiovascular events compared to class comparators

post_marketing_timeline.event: VIGOR trial published — 4x MI rate vs naproxen; attributed to 'naproxen cardioprotection' hypothesis
FDA advisory committee: agrees to add cardiovascular warning to label
Internal Merck memo ('the CV story') — awareness of CV signal internally
APPROVe trial halted early — confirmed 1.92x elevated cardiovascular risk
September 30 — Merck voluntarily withdrew rofecoxib globally

label_at_withdrawal.cardiovascular_warning: Added in 2002: 'Serious cardiovascular events including MI and stroke may occur'

label_data.current_status: VOLUNTARILY WITHDRAWN FROM GLOBAL MARKET — October 30, 2004
label_data.regulatory_history: FDA requested black box warning in 2004; Merck withdrew before it was required

approve_trial.design: Randomized controlled trial — colorectal adenoma prevention
approve_trial.conclusion: Confirmed: doubled risk of serious thrombotic cardiovascular events. Trial halted early for safety.

vigor_trial_signal.note: Early warning dismissed as 'naproxen cardioprotection' — later confirmed to be rofecoxib cardiovascular harm

signal_analysis.benefit_risk_assessment: UNFAVORABLE — confirmed cardiovascular harm in RCT; no unique benefit over existing NSAIDs; no manageable safety strategy possible at population scale
signal_analysis.recommendation: WITHDRAW — confirmed RCT signal, mechanistically plausible, no risk mitigation strategy sufficient at population scale

mechanism_data.primary_mechanism: Selective inhibition of cyclooxygenase-2 (COX-2) → decreased prostaglandin E2 and prostacyclin synthesis
mechanism_data.cardiovascular_mechanism: COX-2 selective inhibition → decreased vascular prostacyclin (PGI2) synthesis → unopposed thromboxane A2 (TXA2) activity → prothrombotic state → platelet aggregation, vasoconstriction, arterial thrombosis
mechanism_data.class_effect: Shared by all COX-2 selective NSAIDs; FDA issued class warning for all COX-2 inhibitors post-Vioxx withdrawal
mechanism_data.why_selective_cox2_risky: Non-selective NSAIDs (e.g., naproxen) also inhibit platelet COX-1 (TXA2 production), providing some cardiovascular protection. COX-2 selective agents lose this protection while maintaining COX-2 prostacyclin inhibition.
mechanism_data.rofecoxib_selectivity_ratio: COX-2:COX-1 selectivity ratio of ~272:1 — exceptionally high selectivity, maximizing cardiovascular risk

key_studies.title: APPROVe Trial: Use of rofecoxib and cardiovascular events
key_studies.finding: 1.92x relative risk (95% CI: 1.19–3.11) of confirmed thrombotic cardiovascular events vs placebo. Trial halted early.

key_studies.title: VIGOR Trial: GI outcomes with rofecoxib vs naproxen
key_studies.finding: 4x higher myocardial infarction rate with rofecoxib vs naproxen (0.4% vs 0.1%). Early warning signal.

key_studies.title: Risk of acute myocardial infarction and sudden death in patients with COX-2 and other NSAIDs
key_studies.finding: Estimated 27,785–55,000 excess cases of serious coronary heart disease attributable to rofecoxib use between 1999-2004

literature_data.regulatory_precedent: Merck voluntarily withdrew rofecoxib globally on September 30, 2004. FDA subsequently issued class cardiovascular warnings for all COX-2 selective NSAIDs. This is a landmark case study in pharmacovigilance failure and post-marketing surveillance.
literature_data.international_actions: EMA, Health Canada, TGA (Australia) all concurred with withdrawal decision.
